brief_document.title: Workflow-management wire service (start/signal/query/cancel/list/describe)
brief_document.purpose: Define the gRPC service and request/response messages for the core workflow-management surface every caller uses. This is the wire form of the engine's start/signal/query/cancel/list/describe API — the contract client SDKs generate stubs from.
brief_document.task: Add workflow.proto declaring a service with StartWorkflow, Signal, Query, Cancel, ListWorkflows, and DescribeWorkflow RPCs and their request/response messages. Each request carries a namespace; inputs and results are aion-core Payload; ListWorkflows carries a WorkflowFilter and returns WorkflowSummary values; DescribeWorkflow returns a summary (and optionally history). Provide the serde request/response types and assert lossless proto<->serde round-trips.

requirements.title: Workflow service definition
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a gRPC service in workflow.proto with StartWorkflow, Signal, Query, Cancel, ListWorkflows, and DescribeWorkflow RPCs, each with a request and a response message.
acceptance: workflow.proto defines a service exposing all six RPCs.
StartWorkflow request carries namespace, workflow type, and input Payload; response carries the assigned WorkflowId and RunId.
Signal request carries namespace, target WorkflowId, signal name, and payload Payload; Cancel request carries namespace, WorkflowId, and reason.
Query request carries namespace, WorkflowId, and query name; response carries a result Payload or a WireError.
tonic-build generates the service stub and the crate compiles.

requirements.title: Namespace, Payload, Filter, and Summary on the wire
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL carry a namespace on every request, carry inputs/results as aion-core Payload, and carry ListWorkflows as a WorkflowFilter returning WorkflowSummary values.
acceptance: Every request message in workflow.proto has a namespace field.
Inputs and results use the common Payload message (from AW-002), not bespoke byte fields.
ListWorkflows request carries a WorkflowFilter (via the AW-002 envelope/conversion) and its response carries a repeated WorkflowSummary.
DescribeWorkflow response carries a WorkflowSummary (history inclusion is optional and, if present, carried as repeated Event envelopes).

requirements.title: Serde types and round-trip
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide serde request/response types for the workflow-management messages that derive Serialize + Deserialize and round-trip losslessly to and from their proto form.
acceptance: Each workflow-management request/response has a serde-deriving Rust type (the generated prost type or a hand-paired serde type per the crate's chosen pattern).
A unit test round-trips at least StartWorkflow, ListWorkflows, and Query request+response through serde_json and through proto encode/decode, asserting equality.
All wire data types here derive both Serialize and Deserialize (CO7) — none is Deserialize-only (these are data, not credentials).

boundaries: SHALL NOT define event-streaming or worker messages — AW-004, AW-005.
SHALL NOT implement the server handlers that call the Engine — that is AW-008.
SHALL NOT add server-side orchestration semantics; this brief defines the wire shape only.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: workflow.proto defines a service with StartWorkflow, Signal, Query, Cancel, ListWorkflows, and DescribeWorkflow RPCs and their request/response messages.
Each request carries a namespace; inputs and results are carried as aion-core Payload, and ListWorkflows carries a WorkflowFilter and returns WorkflowSummary values.
Workflow-management wire messages have serde types deriving Serialize + Deserialize and round-trip losslessly to/from their proto form.

stories.text: As a client SDK author, I want one published wire contract with gRPC service definitions so that I can generate stubs for my language instead of inventing my own request and response shapes.
As a workflow caller, I want to start, signal, query, cancel, list, and describe workflows over gRPC or HTTP so that I can drive the engine from any service without embedding it.

constraints.text: No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a running server gate at runtime (a *_TEST_URL env var with a logged skip), never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion-proto is a leaf crate: it depends on aion-core and external crates only — no aion, no aion-server, no store backend. Structural; must hold (per D2).
aion-server depends on aion, aion-proto, and a store backend among workspace crates; it contains no workflow execution logic — it delegates every durable decision to the Engine (per D5).
Wire data types (proto messages, streamed events, summaries) derive Serialize + Deserialize; config types that carry secrets are Deserialize-only (D4, project credential policy).
Library errors use thiserror (WireError in proto, ServerError in server); anyhow appears only in aion-server's top-level main. No unwrap/expect in library code; lock/stream poison handled explicitly.
No cross-namespace operation reaches the engine: namespace authorisation and scoping happen at the adapter boundary before any Engine call (per D8).
A slow or stalled WebSocket consumer is dropped with a typed lag error; per-connection buffers are bounded; the engine broadcast is never back-pressured by a subscriber (per D6).
No hardcoded operational defaults (ports, heartbeat window, buffer bound, timeouts): all from config; engine-owned defaults deferred to the engine (per CLAUDE.md 'no assumed defaults').
The wire Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary/WorkflowStatus and ID types are aion-core's types, not re-declared wire clones (per D2).

resolved_context.intention: Build the network skin of Aion. The aion engine crate is transport-agnostic by design — no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket — so a CLI embeds it with nothing but a file-backed store. Teams running many services want a managed deployment: one binary clients and workers point at, that streams live workflow events to a dashboard, isolates tenants, and dispatches activity tasks to out-of-process workers in any language. That is aion-server, and the wire contract it speaks is aion-proto. aion-proto is a pure leaf-level contract — gRPC service definitions plus serde wire types — that the server, every client SDK (AL), and every worker SDK (AR) agree on, defined once so they never drift. aion-server is the deployable: it wraps a live Engine, translates wire requests into Engine calls, tails the engine's event broadcast to feed WebSocket subscribers, runs the server side of the remote-worker protocol, partitions everything by namespace, and serves the dashboard's API and static assets. It adds no execution logic — every durable decision still happens in aion.
